value_chain_position: High-speed connectivity silicon (AEC/Active Electrical Cables and SerDes PHY) — sells the active copper cable assemblies and retimer chips that link GPU clusters within AI data centers at speeds from 100G to 800G.
relative_comps: CRDO is the standout growth name in AI interconnect: 3-year CAGR of 93%, YoY of 206%, FCF margin of 21%. Its AEC product is on a genuine S-curve disruption — replacing passive copper DAC cables in GPU-to-GPU links, a function hyperscalers cannot easily internalise. MRVL and AVGO compete at the SerDes/PHY IP layer but CRDO's AEC form-factor is structurally differentiated. At $41B market cap (~30x TTM revenue) the valuation prices in sustained 50%+ growth; any hyperscaler design-win loss would be disproportionately punishing.
